title: The One With The Apothecary Table

lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, everyone is there as Joey enters happily.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen, do you guys think I have a chance with Janine?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, we have been through this before!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Don't do this to yourself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She's made it pretty clear, it is not going to happen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well all right then, I guess I shouldn't get to excited about the fact (excitedly) that I just kissed her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You serious?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah well, we'll see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You kissed her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh we kissed it up real nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you kissed her, so what happened after that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I came over here to tell you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So she's just waiting over there for ya?
Joey nods yes, but suddenly realizes what he did and runs out of the apartment and back to his place.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey and Janine are pretty much making out at one of the tables as Monica and Rachel look on from the couch.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I'm gonna be really late for dance class!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Okay, now I'm really late. (Gets up.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, I'm all right, okay, but hey, could you just leave your lips? (She kisses him on the cheek and starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: (to Monica and Rachel) Bye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye. (Janine exits.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Have you kissed her yet? It's awesome! I could do it forever! Y'know what? She-she kisses better than my mom cooks!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am so glad you said cooks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Oh hey Rachel, sweetheart? You have got to tell the post office that you have moved. Okay? We are still getting all your bills and stuff. (Hands her all of her bills and stuff.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh, Pottery Barn! (Grabs the aforementioned catalog and holds the rest back out to Chandler.) You can throw the rest away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not your garbage man. I'm your mailman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica look! Look-look-look! Here is that table that I ordered. (Shows her the picture.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You got it from Pottery Barn?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! It's an apothecary table. Does anyone even know what an apothecary is?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A pharmacist. (Rachel mocks him.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, Phoebe hates Pottery Barn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate Pottery Barn too. They kicked me out of there just because I sat on a bed!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You took off your pants and climbed under the sheets!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (indignant) I was tired!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe hates Pottery Barn?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, she hates all mass produced stuff. She thinks her furniture should have a history, a story behind it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well this has story behind it! I mean they had to ship it all the way from the White Plains store.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's gotta be one of a kind. Y'know like umm, y'know uh, what's that God awful ceramic fruit bowl she has on her counter?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I made that for her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You made pottery?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I made it of this fruit bowl I found in the garbage.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm telling-If you put that in her apartment you'll never hear the end of it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay fine! I'll-I'll just tell her it's an antique apothecary table, she doesn't have to know where it came from. Oh! Look at this little drawers! Oh look-look it says that it holds 300 CDs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, just like the apothecary tables of yore...
The Hallway between the apartments, Joey, Janine, Chandler, and Monica are returning from a double date. Chandler is telling a joke.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...so then the farmer says, "That's not a cow and you're not milking it." (Everyone laughs.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am so glad you guys got together, Chandler and I are always looking for a couple to go out with and now we have one!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look at us, we're a couple of couples!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I had so much fun tonight, and what a great restaurant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And Chandler I can't believe I let you pay for this one. (They hug and he whispers in his ear.) Thanks man.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So do you guys gonna come over tomorrow? I'll make that pasta thing I was telling you about.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Oh that would be great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, but hey look, at least let us bring the wine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, you don't have too!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope-op! I insist! (He hugs Chandler again and whispers to him.) You get the wine right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah all right. (They part.) Okay, good night guys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See ya tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Can't wait! (They each go into their respective apartments.)
[Cut to Joey and Janine's.]

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: How are we gonna get out of that one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I can't handle two nights in a row with them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what's wrong with Monica and Chandler?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I don't know, they're just a little blah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Blah?!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well y'know, he's blah, she's just-she's very loud for such a small person.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, they're like my best friends. Are you saying we can't hang out with them? 'Cause that would kinda be a problem.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: No! Of course we can still hang out with them. Just y'know, not two nights in a row. Okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Thank you. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you want, I'll sell my friends and use the money to buy you presents.
Phoebe and Rachel's, Rachel is placing CDs into her antique apothecary table as Phoebe returns home.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sees the table) Ooh, what a great table! Where-where did you get it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Guess!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, a flea market?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ha! See, I knew, I knew you'd get it on the first guess. Isn't it cool! It's an apothecary table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Oh you can just imagine that this is where (She's opening and closing the drawers) they kept all the stuff to make their potions.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know? Ooh, you can almost smell the opium.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Almost.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How much was it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was only 500 bucks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 500 bucks at a flea market?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay see I thought, I thought you meant how much was it when it was new, y'know like back then.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah no, I mean it was at a flea market, so it was y'know, it was like a dollar.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A dollar?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And fifty. So it was like one and fifty dollars.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, okay, they gave you the old time pricing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what period is it from?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, it's from yore. Like the days of yore. Y'know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, yes I do. God, oh it's just perfect! Wow! I bet it has a great story behind it too. Did they tell you anything? Like y'know where it was from or...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! That I know, this is from White Plains.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) White Plains. Oh, it sounds like such a magical place.
Ross's apartment, Rachel is entering as Ross comes out of the kitchen carrying popcorn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! We're here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing his new table) Ohh! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You like it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's my new apothecary table!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! Phoebe's gonna be here any second, she cannot see this!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well why not?! She'll-she'll love it! It's the real thing! I got it at Pottery Barn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know you did! I bought the same one! And if she sees your table she's gonna know that I lied to her. I told her ours was an original.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why did you do that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because she hates Pottery Barn.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She hates Pottery Barn?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know! I know, she says it's all mass-produced, nothing is authentic, and everyone winds up having the same stuff. (Ross looks at his table.) So come on, she's gonna be here any second! Can we please just cover this up with something?! Please?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No! No! I am not gonna hide it from Phoebe-Ooh, although I did get some great Pottery Barn sheets! (Gets them.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooo! Oh, I forgot they made sheets!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, yeah! I still can't believe she hates Pottery Barn!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, get over it! It's not like she hates you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah but Pottery Barn! Y'know what I think? It's just she-she's weird. Y'know it's because she's a twin. Twins are weird.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, she's not weird, she just wants her stuff to be one of a kind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh. Y'know what's not one of a kind? A twin!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, Phoebe's here! Okay, let's turn out all the lights and we'll just watch the movie!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Hey-Ooh, cool sheets! (Notices the sheet over the table.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you like it? You wanna know where I got it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He got it a flea market!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You bought your sheets at a flea market? Ross come on, you gotta loosen the purse strings a little.
Joey and Janine's, Joey is pacing around as Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The dinner will be ready in twenty minutes. This is so exciting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And here is the bottle of wine for you to bring over tonight. (Hands it to him.) You were also going to buy Monica flowers but you couldn't afford it, because you paid dinner last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks. Thanks, but uh actually it's just gonna be me again tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened to Janine?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, she's uh-uh really sick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's too bad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, she's been in there all day (Points to her room), uh high fever, a nose problem... Phlegm! Phlegm! Phlegm-phlegm-phlegm!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: (entering from her room) Monica! Chandler! I'm really-really sorry about tonight. I don't know if Joey told you; I just couldn't get out of going to this play. I'm sorry. Have a great time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Kay!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Bye. (Exits)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's funny, I saw no phlegm.
Commercial Break
Joey and Janine's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, she really is sick!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then why-why is she going to a play?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, y'know, starve a fever, go to a play for a cold.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey! Why is Janine not coming over for dinner?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well uh, she didn't want to hang out with you guys two nights in a row. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, why does she not want to hang out with us?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because she uh, she-she thinks that you are blah and that you, Monica, are too loud.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (loudly) What?!! (Quietly) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So she was just pretending to have a good time last night? She was lying to our faces?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, I can not believe this! I mean, who is she to judge us? We could not have been nicer to her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I am not blah, I am a hoot!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! I know! Come on, please-please you guys, don't-don't be mad. I'm sure she just, she just said that stuff because she was nervous and you guys are like my best friends! Y'know? And it was our first date! Plus, she's really sick!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, you sh-No you said you made that up!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, but don't you think the sick thing is way better than the play thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, they're both good. I generally just go with, Monica's drunk again. (Monica glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on you guys, come on please-please just give her another chance, huh? She'll come around I promise.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course we will, come on we gotta make dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
They exit and close the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (from the hallway) I do not like that woman!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shouting) I can hear you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am loud!
Ross's apartment, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe are watching a movie.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Aww! (They all laugh.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's funny. (Phoebe puts her feet up on Ross's table.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, could you please not put your feet up on my new...(On Rachel's glare)...old sheet?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sure! (She goes to take her feet off, but drags the sheet with her which spills the wine.) Noo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My apothecary table!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noooooo!! (Phoebe lifts up the sheet to discover the exact same apothecary table they have.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross, where did you get this?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I got it at Pottery Barn!! Okay?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe, Pottery Barn has ripped off the design of our antique!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Oh my God, well if they've ripped off our table ours must be much more than one and fifty dollars!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well this doesn't even smell like opium.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course not, it smells like wine, which you spilled! And thanks for wrecking my sheet by the way.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Ross, calm down, I'll give you the 80 cents. (Ross glares at Rachel)
Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are sitting at the kitchen table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, one more time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, would you like some more orange juice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Perfect decibel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (loudly) I know!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So uh, what are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh nothing, we're just talking. Y'know, blah-blah-blah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, come on you guys, you said you were gonna try! All right look, I came over here to invite you guys to a movie with me and Janine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I'd like to but, (extremely quietly) I'm not sure we have time to go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ha-ha, very funny-Look! I don't know what to do! I really want you guys to get along. Just please come to the movie with us. I mean you owe me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We owe you?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right! I helped you guys out a lot in the start of your relationship. Huh? I helped you guys sneak around for like six months, and I looked like an idiot! And I was humiliated. And I only made 200 dollars!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We didn't give you any money! (Chandler is motioning, "No!")

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't think I know that!
Phoebe and Rachel's, Rachel is showing Ross around the newly decorated living room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...see I can't decide whether it would go better next to the new wicker dining chair, the Sahara desk, or the Parker console table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! I didn't know that there was a Pottery Barn up here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know. I went a little crazy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A little? Your place looks like page 72 of the catalogue. Oh look at that! The ornamental bird cage! Large!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sees Ross) Oh Ross no. Be careful, that is very old! Okay? Early Colonial bird merchants used to bring their birds to market in that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (glaring at Rachel) Fascinating.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) Another amazing find! Wow! Oh I bet this has a great story too!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it does, it does! It is a room separating apparatus from Colonial times.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm, a lot of this stuff is from Colonial times. Hey, what are some other time periods Rachel? (She glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well there's yore. And uh, y'know, yesteryear.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah no, I'm telling you Rachel has such a great eye for this stuff. Ross, y'know if you ever decide you need to redecorate-And I think that you should. You should, you should ask Rachel to help.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey he doesn't need my help.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, come on! I think he's ready to get rid of, what did you call it? The cheap knock-offs and dinosaur junk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! (To Rachel) Hey, y'know what? Since you have such a great sense of what I need, uh ooh, here's-here's 60 bucks, why don't you take Phoebe down to that Colonial flea market of yours and get me some stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (not sure of what to do) Y'know what? I don't, I don't think Phoebe really wants to come.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! I do want to!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, she does want to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She does want to.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!
The Street, Phoebe and Rachel are heading back from that Colonial flea market.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, I don't know what to say. I guess the flea market was just better last time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well at least I got these sheets for Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
They walk by a Pottery Barn window display.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uck, look at this! Pottery Barn, yuck!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, y'know what? Don't look at it. (Realizes the display is their living room) Seriously, don't look at it. (Tries to pull Phoebe away.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Look-look! There's the coffee table they stole from us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, those bastards! Let's go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That fan kinda looks like ours. And the birdcage and the...wait a sec! This is our exact living room!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! No! No it's not! No it's not! Come on! Phoebe, ours is totally different! I mean we don't have the... (Looks desperately for something different.) We don't have the...that lamp! And-and that screen is y'know, on the other side.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God. This is where you got all our stuff, Pottery Barn! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! Okay-okay look-no I did, I just wanted this stuff and I know how you feel about Pottery Barn. Just... Come on don't be mad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no-no, but I am mad! I am mad! Because this stuff is everything that is wrong with the world! And it's all sitting up in my living room and all I can think about is how I don't have that lamp!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then honey, buy the lamp! Hey, we have that 60 bucks from Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't! I can't! Unless... Well are you saying that-that you would move out if-if I didn't buy that lamp?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! No! I'm not gonna move out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But are you saying that you would move out if I didn't buy that lamp?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gets it) Oh. Yes! I would so move out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay then I don't have a choice! I have to buy that lamp!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's right!
They start to go inside.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But at least the apothecary table is real.
The hallway, Joey, Janine, Monica, and Chandler are returning from their second date.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, this-this-this was great. Didn't everybody have a great time?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well I did. I really did. And you guys, I've got to say, I'm sorry if I was a little weird after the last time we went out. I guess I was just nervous or something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's totally understandable.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: So we can go out again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Oh good.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well then, good night!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good night! (They go into their respective apartments.)
[Cut to Joey and Janine's, they're entering.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See? Eh, wasn't that fun?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: We have got to move!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (bursting in) I knew it!! Y'know, you're not so quiet yourself, missy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I'm blah? Listen, the only thing more boring than watching modern dance is having to listen to you talk about it, (Imitating her) "Oh Chandler, I just lost myself in the moment."

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Y'know, I know you're talking, but all I hear is, "Blah. Blah. Blah-blah-blah."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (steps up and points at her) All right! You and me! Let's go! Right now!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! All right! Enough! Enough! Enough!! Enough! (To Chandler and Monica) You two go home! I-I-I gotta talk to Janine! (They start to leave.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Y'know I think you can take her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Janine) You'd better hope I don't see you in the hallway!! (They exit.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, uh, we've got a little bit of a problem here. These people are my friends; you can't treat them that way.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: They said stuff to me too, y'know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! I know! And I'm going to talk to them about it. They mean so much to me. They... They're like my family. If you guys are gonna be fighting all the time, I-I... I don't think we can be together. It just, it just can't work. It can't. (Starts to break up) I'm very upset.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Would, would it help if I went over and apologized?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah! That would be very helpful! Yeah. (He opens the door for her and she exits into the hallway.)
[Cut to the hallway, Janine is entering as Monica is taking out the garbage.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What did I tell you about the hall?!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I was just coming over here to apologize for my behavior! I'd really like it if we could be friends.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I know that would make Joey happy, so, I would like that too.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now come on. (They hug like men.) Well, I'm glad we worked things out.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I'll see you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye. (Leaves to resume her garbage removal task.)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: (muttering to herself) Or I'll hear you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (hearing that) That's it big girl! (Hits Janine in the butt with the garbage bag) Come on!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Wait! (Janine pauses, then runs down the stairs.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (chasing her) You'd better run!
Joey and Chandler both come into the hallway.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah uh, what am I gonna do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sorry man. (Pause) You wanna go watch?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! (They head downstairs.)
Ending Credits
Central Perk, Ross, Chandler, and Monica are there as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How ya doin'?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey so, did uh, did she move out?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pretty much, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I cannot believe you broke up with her just like that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, when it's not right, you know it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah. I'll be all right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what would cheer you up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm giving this lecture on erosion theories tomorrow night, I think you should come.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughing hysterically) You're right! That did cheer me up!
End
